results.title: OpenAI Reviews (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
Smaller token footprint = more affordable as a daily-driver auditor.

#### vs Alternatives

Claude Code

Actively enforced just running Claude alone for months (cheaper, simpler) and tried Cursor's multi-model setup. Settled on Codex specifically because its reasoning style is different enough from Claude's that it actually catches things rather than agreeing with whatever Claude said.

Two models trained on similar data give you one opinion twice Codex gives you a genuinely independent read.

Shashwat Ghosh

#### What's great

GPT-5.1 represents a meaningful step forward in LLM capabilities. Three key improvements stand out:

1. Engine Segmentation & Personality Presets [...] The Real Unlock: This isn't a revolutionary leap. It's a solid incremental advance that compounds when deployed at scale. The real advantage goes to teams building on top of this—not those claiming AGI is here.

results.title: What Pain-Point are you Solving and How did you discover it?  : Self-Promotion Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Jake Friedberg

@chris\_payne\_emba sounds great & looking forward to it!

### Engineering & Development

### LLMs

### Productivity

### Marketing & Sales

### Design & Creative

### Social & Community

### Finance

### AI Agents

### Trending categories

### Top reviewed

### Trending products

### Top forum threads [...] It has definitely evolved into something bigger than I planned, with a share system and other features. That wasn't in the original plan at all. I've had to keep myself in check though, in order to keep focus.

The most surprising part? The ease of implementing new ideas. Even though a lot of features were built and ultimately scrapped, there's always something you learn. And I'm amazed at how good a tutor AI can be — I'd like to think I learned more in three months than I would have otherwise in five years.

If I were to start over, I'd experiment sooner instead of trying to perfect a core issue. But other than that it's been a terrific ride, really.

Jon Raney [...] Validation: I started by building "low-code" reflection layers for a few leaders I was coaching. When they told me they couldn't imagine going back to their "old way" of disorganized notes and fuzzy expectations, I knew there was a market.

The evolution: It started as a simple reflection tool (Ascent Coach), but it’s evolved into a full-scale "Operating System" suite. I realized leaders don't just need to think better; they need a AI augmented system to execute the human side of the job.

The biggest surprise: How many "world-class" companies are essentially running their most important asset (their people) on legal pads and "vibes."
